Please forgive me if some of the pictures aren't clear, as this was a one woman effort. The length clocks in at about 23 inches. Height is about 26  inches. The width is about 13 inches.

THE ADAPTORS

Youll likely need an adaptor so here goes my illustration. A huge shout out to reviewer @Kat who provided pictures of the type of adapter she used. I would have been lost without her pictures!

I unscrewed my aerator on my kitchen sink, grabbed my intake hose (the skinny unattached one) went to Ace Hardware, asked for assistance right off the bat. The clerk paged a guy from the plumbing area. When the guy came around, I showed him my aerator, the intake hose along with Kats pictures and he said, I got what ya need. Not a problem. He checked to see what type and size my aerator was. It turned out to be a male aerator (Yes there is such a thing as male and female aerator). It clocked in at  inches. He connected the hose barb to the intake hose for me.....awesome guy!!! I asked him if I needed all that other stuff in the bag.(refer to my pictures). He said I only needed the garden hose aerator adapter and the hose barb (thick round, bottle cap looking thing) and that was it. He told me if I wanted it to be a bit more sealed, I could use the round black ring looking thing but it was not necessary. (He was right, it wasn't necessary as I've had no leakage at all).

As you can see from the pictures, you take the barb and pull it through the bottom part all the way up till its nice and snug. You then remove your aerator as demonstrated in the pictures, then attach the aerator adapter, then screw on the barb attached to the hose. By the way, your original aerator is pretty easy to remove. The whole process was pretty easy.

THE PERFORMANCE

The washer fits more clothes than I expected. On my first use, I put in 2 pairs of jean material Capri pants, 4 t-shirts, 4 pairs of underwear and 2 night gowns and it did a good job of cleaning them but tangled them badly. Keep this in mind if you wanna wash delicates! According to the instructions, you fill the washer up just a bit, put some of your detergent in and then let it spin to get the suds rolling. Then you start placing clothes in and continue filling it with water to the desired level. Let me tell you, this machine fills up fast so by no means can you walk away. You really need to watch it. The spinner does a great job ringing the water out. It definitely needs to be balanced or youll have a jumping machine. The machine is mildly noisy but can get very noisy if the spinner part isn't balanced. When its washing, you can definitely relax for those 15 or so minutes. So thats a good thing for sure!

The built in drain pump leaves a lot to be desired. Its so temperamental. I mean sometimes it works then sometimes you gotta help it out a bit and thats what I don't like. I bought this specifically for the built in pump and I expect it to work 100% of the time but unfortunately it doesnt. There seems to be problems with it initiating. You kinda have to wiggle the hose a bit to get it started. Once it starts pumping, it does a great job but the initial phase is the most frustrating.

Also, the intake hose snout detached near the barb adaptor part so I had to buy some gorilla brand super glue and attach it back. So far so good!

THE GOOD, THE BAD AND THE UGLY

Lint is a huge problem. I was stunned by the amount of lint on my clothes once they air dryed. It literally looked like I had mugged and wrestled a couple of muppets. There was definitely no way I could get the lint off with a lint brush. Of course this means, the lint filter is useless. Im not even sure why its there. It never catches anything! Youll need to invest in a floating lint filter or some lint balls. Ive provided pictures of the lint balls and floating lint filter I use. As you can see, there are lots of lint balls in the washertrust me, youll need em all!

I need to add, I just received my magic chef portable dryer and it does a great job of catching the lint. You might want to invest in a portable dryer..they aren't cheap thoughstarting price is about $200.00 but in my opinion its worth it, especially with the lint problem!

Id suggest getting a flat dolly with wheels. Ive attached a picture of the one I have. Itll be a lifesaver if you need to move this washer and dryer around. Its definitely not heavy but itll be less labor intensive if you have a dolly. I often need to move the washer around to get the drain pump working and this dolly really helps.

Id also suggest watching several Youtube videos to see what other owners of portable washers and dryers are doing. Theres lots of great tips out there. For example:

Clean after each use since theres a bit of residue left behind.

Leave the lids open for a full day after washing clothes to prevent mold and mildew.

Tie pantyhose at the end of the drain hose to catch the lint. Or you can purchase lint traps to tie on the end of the drain hose. These are specifically made for portable washers.

Use small laundry bags for delicates since tangling is an issue.

While spinning, add water to the spinner intake part, as this will help rinse a great deal of detergent out. This way, you won't have to rinse as long.

Some people wash, spin, fill the washer back up with water to rinse the detergent out, then spin adding some water and a bit of fabric softener during the spin cycle. Some people skip the rinse part altogether and simply add water during the spin cycle phase to get the detergent out.

My technique doesnt deviate much from what others suggest. I wash for 15 minutes ( I use small laundry bags for anything delicate like underwear). I start the spin cycle, then add cold water in the spin intake valve to get some of the detergent out. I fill the washer back up with cold water, add some fabric softer and rinse for 15 minutes then spin again, sometimes adding a bit of cold water during the last spin cycle phase.

No matter what, there will always be a bit of water trapped in the intake hose and drain hose so please be careful when you're finishing up and taking the hoses out of the sink, bathtub, bucket etc. It's pretty easy draining the trapped water in the intake hose. Just detach from the sink, detach from the washer and hold it up over the sink, bathtub etc.

The drain hose is another story. You'll have to nearly put the drain hose at ground level to get the trapped water to drain. This gets pretty annoying. I push the washer to the top of my stairs, then set a gallon bucket on one of the lower steps and I try to get the drain hose as straight as possible and this usually does the trick. Once again....the dolly comes in hand.

Initially I was doing laundry in the kitchen and draining water in a gallon bucket but this became way too labor intensive. Gallon buckets full of water are heavy and when you have to constantly dump water out, you get tired quick.

I decided to go with the bathtub method. Thank God my bathroom sink's aerator was the same as my kitchen sink's aerator. Phew!!!! I tied a stocking to the end of the drain hose to catch the lint and let the dirty water drain in the bathtub. I like this method better.

By the way when I got this, I had a very difficult time getting the plastic underneath the lid hood off. I had to pop the lid hood off to get the darn plastic that was underneath. It was a serious struggle. Who the heck wraps an appliance like that!!??

THE CONCLUSION

Overall, this is a good washer and dryer. I think it does what it's supposed to do. For it's capacity, (8.8 lbs), I'd say this was perfect for a single person or a couple. I think anything beyond that would require a larger capacity.

The stainless steel spinner is a nice added feature. I think there's only a handful with stainless steel spinners!

The built in pump leaves me frustrated so this is why I'm kinda in limbo about how I feel overall. If I knew how to get good consistently from the built in pump, I'd rate this 5 stars.

Two star deduction for the useless lint filter and inconsistent drain pump!

Side note....check this site out to see how much clothes/towels etc weigh. Make sure you add an additional pounds etc for when clothes are wet. I posted the chart below but it's much prettier on the website (can't post anymore pictures since Amazon limits to 20)......http://www.dummies.com/home-garden/home-improvement/cleaning-stain-removal/how-to-get-cleaner-clothes-by-weighing-them-first/

summary: Very poor performance and customer service
reviewText: I am very dissatisfied with the Whirlpool Cabrio washer.  The water level stays so low that the cleaning is only mediocre and the rinsing is terrible.  Even using an extra rinse cycle, my dark cottons come out with streaks and splashes of white, from the detergent, and look unsightly.  These streaks have to be sponged off by hand with a wet towel or they will still be visible after drying the clothes.  I have tried different detergents--both powder and liquid, as well as different brands.  I have tried different spin speeds and water temperatures.  I have called Whirlpool as well Lowes, where we purchased the machine, and neither offered any helpful advice.  The Whirlpool rep agreed that my machine's performance is unacceptable but offered no remedy and would not transfer me to any higher employee to speak to.  To me, this says that Whirlpool does not stand behind its products, and if a product does not perform well, that's the buyer's problem.  I will not be buying any other products from Whirlpool, including the dryer that would have matched this washer.  In addition to the washing and rinsing problem, the laundry comes out of the washer very wrinkled and hardly wet, due to the high spin speed.  The idea is that this saves energy by reducing drying time.  The purpose is defeated, however, when you have to dampen the clothes before drying, in order to get some of the wrinkles out.  I would strongly advise any potential buyers to steer clear of this machine and all Whirlpool products in general.

summary: Focus at least offered to switch shipping companies for me and that worked like a charm
reviewText:
I bought mine from Focus electronics & Appliances, and they rock !! I tried ordering this from every vendor listed as sellers here and each one cancelled my order after they explained to me that they don't ship to the state of Maine, can you imagine not shipping to an entire state? Focus at least offered to switch shipping companies for me and that worked like a charm.

Now the refrigerator is everything we had hoped for and more. It looks amazing and it works amazing too. I can't say enough about this appliance. We had wanted this for over 18 months, we saved our pennies and finally bought it and we are so happy. Every feature listed works just as expected. We love the "manual ice maker which bty eliminates any spillage, and the door within a door should be standard on every fridge, the express chill zone is awesome and why don't all refrigerators have the express chill function which is great after you've gone grocery shopping. I think Summit has just set the bar a little bit higher for every other appliance manufacturer. One of our crispers was damaged, and one call to Summit late Friday afternoon took care of that. I spoke to humans, they were pleasant and solved my problem right away with a new crisper being shipped out right away.

I want to add that we love waking up in the morning and seeing the green "energy saving" light lit up. One reason we bought this is because the cost of ownership of this fridge, over the long haul is so low. In fact I would venture to say it must be about the lowest for a fridge this big.

I can't say enough good about this appliance except if you don't own this, you need to go out and buy it right away. Don't wait, don't hesitate just go out and buy it, you'll love it.....guaranteed!

reviewText: It gets the kitchen odors out of the house! Other than that, there isn't much to say. I hated installing it. Very flimsy metal and sharp corners for the flange part. Cutting into the shingles of your once perfectly water tight roof is always a big pucker moment! Liberal use of a good roofing sealant and a few nails later and the job is thankfully over. The bird screen is just some metal mesh that is sort of wedged up in there. You would think they could devise some better way of mounting the mesh than "eh, just jam it up in there"... But it keeps the birds out. And if you build homes for a living, please do the world a favor and just add an outdoor vent like this for the range hood!!! Just do it! even if it's one of those silly microwave above the range deals, just run the duct and this vent anyway! even if it's a wall vent. Recirculating vents do not work and no one wants their house full of cooking odors. Seriously. It should just be code.

reviewText: As some who've owned products that this igniter fits likely know, these are typically only good for approximately 12 months for cooks who acually use their ovens.  We've had our Caloric for nearly 20 years now and have replaced igniters like clockwork (well, calendar work? haha) around every 12 months (some haven't gone even 12 months).  While it's hard to say if we've used the oven less this year or not, this igniter has now lasted 16 months and is still firing it up!  It physically matches the majority of others we've purchased over the years, appearance wise, but for whatever reason is exceeding expectations (whether we just just got lucky, or with age somewhat slipped from using it as frequently --which while I don't think is the case, I can't say).

reviewText: These vent covers are meant to be used on the air returns (where air is sucked back to the central system), not the vents where the warm or cool air blows into the room.  In winter you can cover returns that are high on the wall, preventing already-warm air from being pulled back to the furnace.  In the summer, block off the lower returns to prevent cool air from being pulled back to the AC unit.  Since the air is being pulled in through the returns (as opposed to being forced out) these covers have no problem staying put.

reviewText: Replaced the seal on our GE washer with this part as ours had a small tear in it that allowed water to leak out if it was doing a full load. Look online for video instructions on what is involved in replacing so you know what you are getting into, as it is not a casual operation. It is not "hard" but it does require that you disable the washer into several parts by removing many screws in a particular order. Also stretching the wire retaining spring either requires a special spreader tool or in my case the help of my wife to use our four hands to pull this around the unit. Overall the whole operation took less than an hour, and requires a basic socket driver set and the unclipping and reinstallation of several snap on control panel connectors. If you do basic home handy man type projects you should be capable of doing this yourself.

summary: Expensive, but essential to use my fridge. And I survived Toledo's water apocalypse of 2014.
reviewText: First, let me start by letting you know that I live in the Toledo, OH area, and am an indirect customer of Toledo's city water.  Toledo has been very negligent in maintaining their water treatment facility over several decades, and it can't properly filter some of the microcystins that more modern treatment facilities can.  Another toxic algae bloom formed on Lake Erie (a big thanks to the polluters for endangering our water supply), and drifted near the water treatment facility's Lake Erie intake, contaminating the drinking water.  Thanks to our local media, panic ensued, and folks bought up all the bottled water at stores, and water filters.  Restaurants were closed, and many citizens didn't realize that it wasn't a nationwide water apocalypse--you could drive 15 minutes to a town that had it's own water treatment facility and stock up.  When the great water catastrophe was over (3 days), customers had to flush their pipes and appliances.  That's when having a spare filter like this on hand really helped.  Buy a few from Amazon and save over buying them locally, keep a case or two of bottled water handy, and you'll be able to ride out the next water catastrophe in style.  Just beware of those pesky walkers.

summary: Not a direct replacement but can easily make fit
reviewText: As many reviewers noted the cord, when combined with the adapter will not fit.... Or if you manage to jam it in then the ice bucket won't fit.  Simple solution... Remove the two screws that hold the female 4-pin plug to the back side of the freezer. Install ice maker.  Plug in, there is plenty of room in back behind cold air deflector to tuck the cord and the rather large adapter plug. Make sure you don't intrude on the fan or the water feed tube. good idea to unplug unit before you install.  Even less intrusive to the ice bucket than the old one. Installation takes 7 or 8 minutes with a Phillips screw driver. I had to use a large flat blade screw driver to coax the old cord from the connector. Simple twist did the trick.

reviewText: I have owned the Swash machine since late 2014 when it first became available, and it is still going strong. Every week between my husband and I we steam about 15-20 items in it. It isn't meant to clean your clothes. We wash/dry the shirts/pants/skirts and then use this to press them. The clothing come out not quite so crisp as if they'd been dry cleaned or ironed, but they are sufficiently pressed for our purpose. And it seems more gentle on the clothes than if they were ironed. Most of all, it saves time and money. If mine broke tomorrow I wouldn't hesitate to purchase another in a heartbeat.

summary: Good compact fridge, although the door reversing process could be designed a bit better
reviewText:
Finding a small 2.5 cubic foot compact refrigerator is not as easy as it sounds.  And finding one with a stainless steel-style door is even harder.  This was literally the only selection on Amazon that met those specifications.

While I had not heard of the brand before (SPT), I ordered this as replacement for my aging 2.5 cubic foot fridge.  I am mostly pleased with the purchase.  It is compact enough to fit under a desk, and is much quieter than the previous model it is replacing.  It is also substantially lighter weight, although at around 48 pounds (shipping), it isn't exactly a feather-weight either,

At this size and price point, you don't get many features.  There's no interior light (but you don't need one anyway), and the freezer compartment is very small - just big enough for a small ice cube tray.  But as a second fridge or for an office or game room, it's more likely to hold sodas, beer and snacks anyway.  However, there are three features that are an improvement over my old compact fridge:

1) Convenient Temperature Control - My old fridge had one setting - "ON".  The SPT has a dial on the inside (on the upper right corner) that allows you to select the level of cold you need.  Nice.

2)  Adjustable Feet - There are two screw-type adjustable leveling feet, so the fridge will accommodate just about any minor irregularities on your floor.

3)  Mostly Solid Back - Older models often exposed the coils, but the SPT has a mostly closed case design.  There is an opening to allow air circulation around the compressor and to access the electrical cord.

I would have given this 5 stars, but ultimately deducted 1 star for the somewhat twitchy process of reversing the door. It ships with a right-hand swing, but you can reverse that by moving the hinges to the other side.  This could have been designed a little better and takes some fiddling to get everything to fit just right.  Part of this entails removing plastic caps covering the mounting holes on the reverse side, which are tricky to pry out.  Use a small blade screwdriver and take care not to scratch the cabinet.  One of those plastic caps broke fairly easily and I feared I'd have to glue it in place.  But SPT took the added - and much appreciated - step on including an extra packet of plastic caps for just such an issue.  Well done.

In short, this SPT fills a rather specific need in a simple and relatively inexpensive fashion.  I look forward to using this little fridge for a long time.

reviewText: Have owned this unit for a bit over 3 yrs. Yes, it's a great beer fridge & we used much like an ice chest for parties; so it got heavier use than average. Many of the issues of the 3 star reviews are right: the outsides get hot in Summer (almost enough to fry an egg on), it doesn't feel like it fully closes sometimes, it's been difficult to find sweet spot on the temp control dial. Recently had an issue where the unit stopped cooling effectively; even at coldest setting it only got to 40. Called about having it repaired; it would cost as much as I paid for it. I unplugged it figuring we'd be getting a new one. For some reason I plugged it back the next morning, within an hour it was back to new again = it had to reset = an electronics issue. Overall, a decent (larger) compact fridge at a decent price. I recommend a refrigerator/freezer thermometer (Taylor makes a good one). Expect some issues. Giving it 3 + 1 stars b/c this little fridge "redeemed" itself.

reviewText: Like others, I am frustrated with my purchase.  First, the clothes sit in the dryer for just over 2 hours and are not dry.  They claim this is to increase the life of the clothes and save on energy.  Second, the clothes bunch-up and get tangled.  I have never had this problem in my life, but I find myself having to stop this dryer regularly and unbunch the clothes, particularly sheets.  Third, in the two years I have owned it I have had things break.  The latest in the "drain light", which means the dryer stops due to an electronically registered error; I haven't fixed it yet, but there's no easy answers to be found on the web and Bosch website is useless.  The call center was nice, but had no information on the problem or even what sensor triggers the light.  Must have a service rep make a $100 house visit for starters, is their answer.

reviewText:
I received this product as a free model for review from Ambient Weather.  I'll be honest, when I first opened the package, the first thought that came to my mind when I held the meter in my hand was that it felt "cheap".  I continued to have that thought in my mind as I worked with the meter over a few weeks and then I realized, I was only thinking that because it was very lite...but that's one of the great things about it.  It's not a big bulky meter.

I'm a SkyWarn spotter and I was looking for a nice pocket sized meter to use when I'm out and about.  I tried one of those meters that you plug into the jack on your phone but it took to long to pull it out of it's case, plug it in, open the app... you get the idea.  This meter comes with a lanyard you can put around your neck as well as a pouch you can put on your belt.  It's also small enough that you can just put it in your pocket (it easily fits in your hand).

I found the meter to be quite accurate out of the box.  The display is nice and easy to read, with a convenient back-light.  You can easily switch between data modes.  One feature I like is the auto-off feature.  When I'm out spotting, I'm gonna pull the meter out, take the readings and report them right away, I'm not going to mess with buttons before I put the thing in my pocket so it's nice to know that it will power down on it's own.  I found myself using it on a few days where we had high winds and it was great to be able to report measured speeds as opposed to estimated speeds.

I even found myself using this to help balance my central heating unit in my home.  I could tell what the difference in blower speeds at each duct.

Could improvements be made on it?  Sure, but can't everything be improved somehow?  Overall, I think this is a nice, low-cost meter.  I could easily recommend this to fellow SkyWarn spotters.

summary: Fits well and solved slow to heat  problem in my Maytag oven.
reviewText:
The problem began months ago with my Maytag oven: it began taking longer and longer to reach my set point temperature. I took a crap shoot and ordered the cheapest and easiest part in the heating circuit, the "Ignitor".
 It was either that and/or the "Bi-Metal Gas Valve". The ignitor was easy (for me) to change, and only required cutting the stock oven plastic connector off and stripping the wires for the (included) ceramic wire nut connections. Polarity does not matter.
 This part is for 120 volt systems and has a universal mounting bracket for various burner designs. The old part had a silver sticker on the wires that, through Google and Amazon, led me to this part. It certainly worked for me!
 I was was quoted by an appliance repair guy 4 times what I paid just for the part AND there would have been 2 trips and 2 labor charges (at [...]per hour) by the service tech plus shipping time and expense!
 After testing, the oven burner reliably comes on after about 70 seconds- a major improvement!  Total time including disassembly and reassembly: 45 minutes. Major savings over an appliance repair house call.
 Baked goods! Life is good.
text:
Fits well and solved slow to heat  problem in my Maytag oven.. The problem began months ago with my Maytag oven: it began taking longer and longer to reach my set point temperature. I took a crap shoot and ordered the cheapest and easiest part in the heating circuit, the "Ignitor".
 It was either that and/or the "Bi-Metal Gas Valve". The ignitor was easy (for me) to change, and only required cutting the stock oven plastic connector off and stripping the wires for the (included) ceramic wire nut connections. Polarity does not matter.
 This part is for 120 volt systems and has a universal mounting bracket for various burner designs. The old part had a silver sticker on the wires that, through Google and Amazon, led me to this part. It certainly worked for me!
 I was was quoted by an appliance repair guy 4 times what I paid just for the part AND there would have been 2 trips and 2 labor charges (at [...]per hour) by the service tech plus shipping time and expense!
 After testing, the oven burner reliably comes on after about 70 seconds- a major improvement!  Total time including disassembly and reassembly: 45 minutes. Major savings over an appliance repair house call.
 Baked goods! Life is good.
label: Positive

summary: Finally a realistic alternative to OEM price gouging.
reviewText:
After trying an imported junk filter, and having to return it. And THEN in-a-pinch having to pay full-pop for the mega-profit gougy OEM, I decided to try this one.

This appears to be made by a REAL USA based filter company that has a real website. That was reassuring.

Commenters talk about leak fears. But my expectations were a bit higher than that. Not only did I of course want a filter that doesn't leak, but I ALSO wanted a really good water filter at a realistic price.

This Water Sentinel filter appears to be exactly that.

They also included a small tube of O-ring lubricant which I thought was a nice touch.

The water tasted pure and great after the initial "flush". And that has continued for the two weeks or so until now. But one thing that I also noticed a few days after installation and use: I looked into the ice cube storage, and the more recently produced cubes were noticeably clearer than the older cloudier ones below them, that had been produced with the barely-ready-to-be-replaced pricey OEM filter.

I'm not sure if the bad reviews of this product are from agents of Asian competitors, or if real people actually had less than positive experiences with these filters. All I can say is that I am a real reviewer and user of this filter. I really used this filter in our Kenmore Elite side-by-side. And it is our new go-to replacement filter.

Moreover, while some of the photos shown would (proportionally) appear to be the original thickness what Ive received from several retailers are the thinner filters.

For a great discussion thread on this topic and instructions on how to buy the original filters (HAC-504OS, $12.99 ea) see http://www.amazon.com/review/R21QW3XX459H67/ref=cm_cr_pr_cmt?ie=UTF8&ASIN=B000RDAORO&linkCode=&nodeID=&tag=#wasThisHelpful (scroll up to see the original comment by CTB.)
text:
Beware of crappy filters being sold under the same HAC-504 part number!. As so many other commentators have posted, the original filter thickness was 1.25 and now much of what is being offered at Amazon instead ships thinner filters (0.75) which are GROSSLY  inferior  See the difference at [...] . reviewText: My front load HE washer sits on the first floor of an old farmhouse - read, nothing is level! For years it walked, I repositioned it; it walked again, I repositioned again, and so on. We recently remodeled and built a closet for the washer and dryer. Before we had a chance to install the doors, I did two loads of laundry and it walked 4" out of the closet, right past the door frame. Clearly, this wouldn't work, so in desperation, I ordered the anti-walk silent feet. Even with the positive reviews, I was skeptical, but to my amazement the washer hasn't walked - at all, as far as I can tell - after at least 20 loads of laundry. There's still some vibration, but not as much, and the major problem, walking, has been solved. If this can work in my house, it should work anywhere.

reviewText:
Our GE (made by Samsung) was purchased in Aug of '08 while moving into a new house.

Pros:  Good looking, good storage space and features, keeps consistent temps.

Cons:
1).  The compressor motor was not plugged into the wiring harness at the factory, so it took two weeks for GE to send somebody out to fix.  Luckily we were moving in from out of town so didn't lose any food or $.
2).  The ice maker died last summer and took out the main motherboard with it.  Our 2 year extended warranty was over, so it cost $400 in parts.  I replaced everything myself and avoided labor cost.
3).  This past fall I noticed water building up in the bottom of the fridge.  I finally tracked the problem to a frozen drain hole in bottom of the interior cooling coil area.  You have to remove all the shelves, pull the back panel off (inside the fridge, not the back panel), use a hair dryer for an hour to thaw the frozen plug, then wrap some bare copper around the defroster unit and into the hole so it won't freeze shut again.  Luckily I did that myself and it did not ruin the fridge or cost me $.

I would look for an option with higher customer rating or buy the longest extended warranty you can find.

reviewText: I installed the icemaker, read the suggestions, but it never filled with water.  It is supposed to fill when you first cycle it on but it did not.  Waited 24 hours like some others suggested but still no water fill.  Thought it might be the water valve but was tired of messing with it and finally called a repairman, which I am glad I did.  Turns out there was a short in the ice maker and while it would cycle, no power/signal was being sent to the water valve and the ice maker had to be replaced. reviewText: My Samsung refrigerator strictly states to use only these Samsung refills for the water filter and I am sure happy that I listened. The water filter works perfectly and comes with instructions and stickers with a month on them for you to stick the corresponding sticker to the filter as a reminder to change the filter in 6 months. Very easy to remove filter and replace it. Very helpful and detailed instructions as well. After replacing the filter, I dumped out the first 3 gallons of water to pass through the filter, along with the first 3 ice trays it made per the instructions. Now when I get ice water from the fridge, it tastes amazing, is nice and cold, and best of all it is crystal clear! My last fridge I had placed a popular brand name filter in it and every time I used the ice water, you could see what looked like sediment floating around and almost cloudy near the ice cubes. Since I recently purchased this fridge and changed the filter though, I have not seen any cloudy water nor any sediment or particles. This filter truly does keep your water clean and clear and tasting great!

reviewText: I'm trying to love this stove because it's the most expensive thing in our house and it is so beautiful.  I'm not sure what stove the reviewer was talking about who said it's "pinpoint accurate" because the control knobs don't have any point on them that indicate where the temperature is set.  The end of the knob is about 1/2" wide and it's nearly impossible to set it to any temperature and get it to stay there.  I use an oven thermometer and still have to babysit the oven.  I have never yet in six months baked anything that didn't completely char to black on the bottom while the top isn't brown yet.  It's SO disappointing; the oven is practically useless.  Yes, you can set the stove knobs to low (simmer) but they will shut off.  It's true that the stove knows to also shut off the gas, which is great because your house won't explode, but the stuff on the stove will stop cooking!  When I called Bertozzoni they said this is normal and you have to use the additional simmer burner if you want a low simmer.  They also told me that the oven needs at least 30 minutes to warm up to prevent burning.  Even though the thermometer says it's at temperature after ten minutes, I wait a half hour but still everything burns on the bottom.  I would rate it a zero but I'm giving it two stars for it's looks and because I hope someday I'll read something that tells me how to get it to work correctly now that I'm stuck with this thing for the rest of my life.

summary: and the water ran dirty as was expected
reviewText:
It took me 96 hours to do one load of laundry.

I was trying to do a simple, medium sized load of underwear and socks (this is equal to about 1/4 of what one standard, industrial washer could handle at a laundromat). The wash cycle takes 2-2:30 of tumbling by hand, and the water ran dirty as was expected. Then comes the rinse cycle, another 1:30 of tumbling with only water. The water is supposed to run clean at this point, but it ran dirty. No big deal, I thought. Underwear should be dirtier than shirts. So I run the rinse cycle again, another 1:30 of tumbling by hand. Still the water runs dirty. One more try. Still the water runs dirty. One more try....still dirty!!!! It took five rinse cycles of 1:30 each, and one tumble cycle of 2:30, for it to finally run clean. That's 10:00 total just spent spinning the handcrank. Draining the water takes another minute each time, so add six minutes to that for 16:00. Then you have to wring out the clothes, or they're too wet to dry and risk becoming moldy. Add another 3:00 for that. With a 1-minute setup to the machine, that means 20:00 for one medium sized load of underwear, all done with a hand crank.

Then came the drying. These clothes come out *very* wet -- much more than your standard washing machine. Regular, fruit-of-the-loom underwear and hanes socks took 4 days to air dry in a fully ventilated apartment. Four days!! I air dry my clothes, even when using a washing machine, and I've never seen anything come out so wet it takes that long to dry.

So, all in all, it took 96 hours to do one minuscule load of laundry. If I had tried to do one full load of underwear, what a regular washer could handle at a laundromat, it would have required doing that about 4 times, or an hour and twenty minutes just spent cranking and draining and wringing out clothes.

NOT WORTH IT!!! One star, only because it technically does get the clothes clean, albeit after a herculean effort.

reviewText:
This is a very good fridge, hence the 4 stars. It worked perfectly in an undercounter setup for us for roughly 18 months, no complaints.
Apparently, though, these undercounter fridges (at least the ones under the $2k mark) all just have some reliability issues. There was another brand we purchased 3 years ago and the compressor died before our 1 year manufacturer's warranty was up; the company, however, said fixing these things was too expensive, so instead they told us to trash it and they issued us a refund. When this Kalamera fridge died on us, though, it was outside of the warranty, so I was concerned we'd just have to swallow whatever costs we encountered. I emailed the company to see if they had any local repairers they'd recommend, and they initially sent us some new power boards to install to see if we could resolve the issue ourselves. When that didn't work, they issued us a refund asap. To me, this is unheard of outside of a warranty period. I liked the company's responsiveness, as well as how they financially stood behind their product. I'd give them five stars for customer support and service, but the product itself 4 stars.

Which I have high doubts about the reviews that claim it doesnt work, they probably tried to DIY or didn't have someone entirely knowledged or with sound background to install it.

That or the piece they got was malfunctioned to begin with.  Otherwise, it went in fine. Works fine. Heats just as fine and clothes are dry, smell great and yeah, it is definitely worth the cheap price.

Only issue I had was shipping. I had to reschedule my technician to come a day later but considering I saved hundreds of dollars by not buying a new dryer, I am happy.

Will probably order this again.

reviewText: Last year I replaced one of the elements on my Frigidaire stove with a generic element. That one lasted about a year and never got hot quickly or hot enough to boil water. They are really not that much more expensive than the generic brands but they work so much better. My stove is in excellent condition, so dents or scratches, so I wanted it to look great and work great. I'm very pleased with these elements. They seem sturdy and well made like my originals and heat up quickly.

This review is for Samsung DA29-00003G Compatible Refrigerator Water Filter by MORE Pure Filters, MPF16025.

I ordered Samsung DA29-00003G Compatible Refrigerator Water Filter by MORE Pure Filters, MPF16025 to replace filter for my Samsung fridge. So far, everything seems to be working fine. Water is tasting like it supposed to and I have no issues whatsoever. I like the price of this product as it is significantly less than buying from the manufacturer.
Warning: Once installed, make sure that you drain at least two gallons of water before drinking from your fridge!!!!!!
Also, make sure that you check the model of your fridge before buying. Her is the models that it is compatible with:
The MORE Pure MPF16025 is compatible with the following filter part numbers:
DA29-00003G
DA29-00003
DA29-00003A
DA29-00003A-B
DA29-00003B
DA61-00159
DA61-00159A
DA61-00159A-B
DA97-06317A
EFF-6011A
HAFCU1
HAFCU1/XAA
HAFIN1
HAFIN2/EXP
RFC1100A
SGF-DSB30
TADA29-00003A
TADA29-00003B
W10132126
WF289
WFC2201
WSS-1

5 Stars: EXCELLENT!!! The product met and exceeded all of my expectations. Nothing needs improvement. Highly recommended.
4 Stars: Met most, but not all of my expectations. There is at least one little thing I do not like or needs improvement.
3 Stars: Average/Good. The product gets the job done, but barely meets my expectations. Not great. Something or a few things need improvement.
2 Stars: Don't bother wasting your time or money. The product does not meet the designed purpose.
1 Star: Forget it! It is a complete failure and was a complete waste of money! Go find another product.

I have received this product at a reduced price or free for my honest review. I have not promised nor was asked to make it a good review. My review is based upon the rating above and not influenced by a discount that I received.

summary: Make less noise than other types and really work
reviewText:
I love these things.  For some time, we have used dryer balls to decrease wrinkles and drying time.  They work.  I started with the completely round and harder ones, the ones with little bumps all over them, but they made a lot of noise and broke in half after some time.  This design makes much less noise and cannot break.  And yes, they decrease wrinkles and drying time.  For reals.  I use five or six of them in the dryer all the time.

The only problem I have with any dryer balls is keeping them in the dryer.  They have a habit of sneaking into pockets, long sleeves, and the corners of fitted sheets.  This being so, I had to buy four or five sets and only have five or six in the dryer at one time; they others are hiding in large blankets and show up on their own, sneaky little things.

Yes, you should buy these if you they are in stock.

summary: Good filter. Fits as expected. OEM quality and cheaper than ordering from local stores!
reviewText:
Exactly as described. Fits into our refrigerator as expected.

For people out there that think that air filtration is a gimmick, it's not. These filters do work and make a big difference, especially if you're the type to save leftovers or eat exotic foods. We went a few days without it and already noticed more food smell upon opening the door. And if you must have more proof, remove your old filter, smell it, it will smell awful (rotten food), which is pretty much proof that these filters actually work.

P.S. Remember to remove the tape from the filter. You don't want to block the air from circulating.

reviewText: My Kenmore dishwasher quit drying the dishes AGAIN after a replacement Kenmore part failed after several months. I ordered this part instead of the required Kenmore part because this was much cheaper. This has worked great for my Kenmore Dishwasher model 587.15252402. Yeah I know, this part isn't called out for that dishwasher, but if you know what you're doing, it will work. Basically all these dishwasher thermostats are roughly the same. There are slight variances in the cutoff temperature, the size of the head, and the size of the electrical terminals. Other than that, they're just a temperature sensitive switch. I changed the female spade connectors on my dishwasher to fit this thermostat, and [carefully] bent the terminals down at a slight angle like my original thermostat, then installed. My dishwasher has been working fine for 5 months now.

summary: Easy DIY solution to a non-functioning oven
reviewText:
Purchased this when my oven suddenly decided to not bake things. The burners worked and that was about the only thing that did. When searching for a reason why my oven stopped working it led me to replacing the oven control board. A search on Amazon and inputting my stove's model number resulted in this board being recommended.

Installation was very easy and took about 5-10 minutes to complete. 7 screws to remove the back panel of the stove, 4 screws to remove the non-working control board, use of a pliers to grip the edge of the old overlay to remove it from the old control board, reapplication of the old overlay to the new control board, and reassembly of the oven.

One piece of advice, take a picture of the wiring locations on the old control board prior to removal or simply unplug them one at a time while immediately connecting them to the new board. There's 5 wires along the right side of the board that you DO NOT want to get mixed up. I used a pliers in removing the old button overlay and this made it extremely easy. The adhesive remained mostly intact and allowed it to be placed on the new board with some security. The overlay was further secured when I mounted the new board to the stove with the 4 screws. Some will state that you need an entirely new overlay but I would hold off on buying this $15-20 part until you know that your old one will not work.

My oven is now fully functioning for a fraction of the cost to have a repairman come out and do it.

summary: all sensors and thermo switches/fuses tested good as did the igniter
reviewText: Spent 5 hours trying to figure out why igniter would not glow, all sensors and thermo switches/fuses tested good as did the igniter. Checked and cleaned Motor switch and timer switch contacts with emery paper. Still no glow.  Turned out timer switch was not making contact from A to C contacts when checked with ohm meter.. Contacts looked good. Turned out I had to give the A contact a slight bend and now it works normally. It took me a while to finally figure it out but now I know my Dryer inside out.  I rated this as 3 stars because I really cant rate it but hope anyone considering this item first check Igniter resistance Mine was 60 ohms and then check your timer contacts to make sure 120v is getting supplied to the circuit. No where on the web did anyone mention the timer as cause for a dead igniter. Hope this saves a few people some time.

summary: An excellent replacement part and good value
reviewText:
My family and I are caretakers for a newly acquired property. We have found many items on the 35 acre property that have been severely neglected. Among the neglected, was a KitchenAid KUIS185JSS ice maker. It had severe scale build up, mold in the internal lines and a water pump that was working, but gross inside. Most would have simply thrown the ice machine away, but at $2000 new, I figured $300 worth of new parts and a little elbow grease was worth the risk.

I originally ordered all my parts through Sears Parts Direct and all arrived new except for the pump. It was used and in no better condition than the one I already had. Be warned if you decide to go this route! So, fearing that Sears would send me another in the same condition, I turned to Amazon... glad I did! I found it here for $30 less and it arrived in new condition. It was identical in every way to the original and stamped with a genuine Whirlpool part number. No way to know for sure if it's a genuine Whirlpool part, as there are lots of fakes these days, but it bolted right up and works great. It's quiet and pumps water like it's supposed to. I can't attest to it's longevity as it's only been a few months, but I expect it to last for years. We only use the ice maker for the summer and will shut it off in the winter, so even if we have to replace the pump every season, it's still cheaper than purchasing a new machine for the next several years. Time will tell and I will hopefully remember to update my review this time next year when we fire it up again.

summary: Perfect replacement for the filter in my Samsung refrigerator
reviewText:
Perfect replacement for the filter in my Samsung refrigerator.  It took a bit of research to make sure I was getting the right filter as there were several models with different part numbers listed that supposedly worked in this application.  Fortunately this one worked perfectly even though it had a different part number.

Installation:

Replacing these filters literally takes seconds.  Screw out the old one and screw in the new one.  Wipe up a few drops of water that spills and you're done.  Don't forget to reset the reminder light on the refrigerator.  Then run at least a gallon of water through the filer to flush out any impurities from the manufacturing process.  I used a large water glass that I refilled several times and dumped in the sink.  The filtered water tastes great  (no chlorine, etc.) and seems to flow faster than the original filer that was probably starting to clog up from filtering so much water..

The only con is that the Samsung OEM filters are expensive for what they are and have to be replaced every 6 months.  I would give it 5 stars except for the price.

summary: WOW it really is a great cleaner
reviewText: when you open the box and get to the square shaped all white box make sure when you open it to look for the little allen wrench with two small screws. they are used to keep the brush head attached to the rod.  The rods are flexible to let you guide the brush through an L shaped bend. The longer brush is great for cleaning out the dryer vent where your lint catcher is. After using it the first time I went right over the neighbors home to ask him to come over to my house for him to actually see how well this kit cleans out the dryer vent. Wash is done one day a week. but at his home he is doing laundry on a daily basis, two adults three kids. Offered for him to borrow it when he has the time to clean his own dryer vents out. He was also shocked / impressed at how well it works. Just remember to read the little paper work which is attached to the bag that hols the rods. = KEEP the drill in clockwise spinning mode. ATTACH the bigger brush and use the little screw with allen wrench to tighten it onto rod. IF you do not take the time to attach the brush correctly it could come free from the rods while in the vent tube and then you will have to take your time to get the brush head out. Also the paper has info that you could use duct tape where the rods attach to one another to make sure the rods do not unattach from each other. For me I just kept the drill in clockwise rotation and had to problems. The spinning brush really does a great job at moving the lint off the dryer vent tube. I did two passes through the tube, a third time seemed unnecessary as I could clearly look into the vent tube and see with my own eyes that the tube was very much free from lint. Just move the rods far enough to reach the end then bring the rods all the way out, it will spin and push the lint out both ways, to the out side of your home and then back to the inside of your home. you have to clean a little lint off the floor but way better than having the risk of a fire start form having it stay in the vent tube itself. this vent really makes dryer vent cleaning easy, both in a job well done and in a time saving manner.

reviewText:
It's a non-ducted hood, so it's only going to be so good since it can't vent outside.

Size: I checked a bunch of range hoods out at a local store before settling on this one.  I was disappointed that even the higher-end models at three times the price didn't fully cover the entire oven like my old one did - it's the same case with this Broan model.  This one's lip is very short and only covers a bit more than half of the range, instead of the entire range (on a standard 30" oven/range), which makes it pretty useless for capturing smoke & steam from the front burners.

Noise: Yes, it's noisier than my old one (and somewhat flimsier, but it's not like I ever touch anything other than the fan & light switch on it, so whatever).  I can't tell if the fan really works because smokey stuff (on the back burner, which actually goes up into the capture area) still stinks up my kitchen.  The fan noise is fairly obnoxious too, it sounds like it's been installed for ages & is old instead of brand new, if you've ever heard a well-used fan before.

Filter: After reading through the reviews, I did buy the five-dollar charcoal filter.  The stock one is a blue one and the one that Amazon suggests has what appears to be a black carbon filter on it.  I can't tell if it makes a difference between it still smokes out my kitchen because the lip only covers half the stove, and even the stuff that is filtered still blows smokey air into the room, so what good is it?  What a dumb design, to not fully cover a standard-size stove - I would have gotten the more expensive one with the dual filters & better lights, but that one covered the same area, was three times more expensive, and there aren't really any other good non-ducted options on the market that I could find easily in-store or on Amazon.

My lightbulb shorted out when I turned it on the third day in, so I don't know if that's a defective or just a random coincidence.  It was fairly easy to install, although the holes were different than my old one & I had to get a drill to punch holes where the wood lip was on my upper cabinets.  That was kind of lame.

Overall, disappointed, but I more or less knew what I was getting into.  For the price, it is what it is.  It doesn't look horrible, at least, and it's slightly better than having nothing to funnel the air in at all, I guess.  It was super easy to install, aside from the some of the holes (in particular the front right) not lining up with my (standard-sized) cabinets.  I was hoping it was going to be better than it was based on the the high numbers of stars & reviews.  Oh well.

summary: Graphic switch overlay failure due to cheap material
reviewText: Our Zephyr Hurricane range hood is fine, but I am disgusted with the graphic overlay on the membrane switch on the front of the unit. Clearly, they used cheap polycarbonate, which has failed after seven years. My husband and I both come from an industry that involves membrane switch and overlay manufacture, and we know too well what a cheap film can do to the appearance of an appliance. That's why we promoted the use of polyester rather than polycabonate for graphic overlays. The on/off dome on ours has split and is about to fall off. It's an aesthetic problem, but one that should not be occurring on a unit that costs nearly $600. But even a standard polycarbonate dome should survive 250,000 actuations. By our calculation, ours has not even had to withstand one third of that. Very poor choice of materials, I'd say. If the overlay had been manufactured with polyester film, it would likely outlast the life of the appliance.

summary: so they recommended this part- which fits
reviewText:
Got this for my Maytag QuietSeries 300 dishwasher with a broken handle.  Packaging and delivery from Amazon was superb, and product was a genuine Whirlpool part.  My dismay is with Maytag - the exact part I needed for my particular model did not include the handle, so they recommended this part- which fits, but not exactly.  The handle is a bit too big for the opening, and will only close fully with some added pressure.  I discovered this after installation - all the buttons worked except "start."  After initial panic that I did not install it correctly, I realized that the handle was not completely closed, preventing the dishwasher from starting.  After applying some pressure to the handle to fully close it, it started right up.

For DIYers, the installation was simple.  The biggest challenge for me was removing the electrical connectors from the latch assembly. The connectors were very tight and would not come apart easily for me, and I was afraid of applying too much pressure with the pliers because the connectors are cheap plastic.  I ended up breaking off the old connector while leaving the dishwasher connector intact.

reviewText:
OK, I admit, most of the problems I had installing this unit were my fault.  I bought it not thinking that it is intended to be a REPLACEMENT unit, not an original installation.  As a REPLACEMENT, i.e., swapping out a defective unit for this one, you will have all the extra parts (ice bucket, etc.) that I had to get separately.  If that is your intent you will probably not have any problem with the installation.  I originally intended to return this one but decided to face the challenge and now I'm glad I did.  Now that it's in it works like a champ!  After increasing the water flow the ice cubes are coming out beautifully, and the unit is much faster than I expected.  Filled my fabricated ice bucket that I purchased at the 99 Cent store in a day and a half.  The only complaint I have is that the instructions are woefully inadequate.  Also, I think the supplier should point out that the unit is intended for replacement, not original installation.  Other than that I am very happy with it.

One more thought.  Some previous reviews complained that some of the rear cubes were not ejecting properly and jamming up the unit.  I experienced that as well until I increased the water flow to what I think is about max.  No problem after that.

Would highly recommend it for as a replacement.

reviewText:
Is ALWAYS off balance whenever you wash anything of weight (ie: towels, sheets, blankets etc.). It takes me close to 2 hrs to get these items washed because I have to keep starting and stopping it to try to get it to spin dry because it ALWAYS throws the items so is off bal. and won't spin. Then when you hit pause, adj. bal., & start it again, it starts REWASHING the items (instead of continuing on from where you left off). HORRENDOS Machine!
NONE of the cycles will get any of my clothes clean...it just uses so little water your clothes come out feeling grundgy and still dirty. I have had to resort to ONLY using the BULKY cycle because it is the ONLY cycle that will use a higher water level (mainly because bulky items like towels will absorb the water so it needs to add more). With this cycle at least my clothes don't FEEL dirty when they are done washing!
There is no way of knowing how much time is left on any of the cycles, which is a real problem when you have to start and stop it, because you really have no idea how far back it goes to start itself up all over again. There is just no way to time it, because it keeps messing up where you have to keep stopping/starting it, and then you have no idea of time frame. Never saw a machine built so UNUSER friendly!
I will NEVER buy a MAYTAG washer again, and NEVER will I buy a washer unless it HAS the center agitator, and a control for water level. These HE washers are the worst, and unfortunately they are now the only washers being made, but without water, clothes just DO NOT ever get clean anymore...no matter which 'fancy' and expensive detergent you use. HE washers are HORRIFIC....I may just buy an old style 'used' washer with the agitator...at least I won't be wasting energy to only wind up with STILL dirty clothes when using HE washers! FINAL NOTE: AVOID Maytag HE Washers at ALL COST!....you will be sorry if you decide to purchase one!

reviewText: I bought this item as a replacement for my Kitchenaid ice maker part#W10190978.  Their product description says it is the same "complete" item.  Unfortunately, it is neither the same or complete.  The water receptacle does not have an opening for my freezer's incoming water line.  The biggest problem is that the supplied power cord is 6" too short since the Kitchenaid freezer has its plug on the other side of the unit. I pulled pieces off my old unit to try to make this work, including the electrical cord, but when all is plugged in and in place, the unit isn't doing anything.  Wish I could send it back.  Very disappointed.
